bullet_points: Review the general form of the Horndeski action as described in arXiv:1404.3713.
Summarize the classical (tree-level) stability conditions for tensor and scalar perturbations (no-ghost, no-gradient instability).
Identify the constraints imposed by the speed of gravitational waves ($c_T^2=1$) and other observational requirements.
Clearly state all assumptions made in the underlying background metric (e.g., FLRW, gauge choices) to ensure a consistent framework for the quantum analysis.
Outline the background equations and perturbation structure relevant for subsequent quantum analysis.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Classical Stability Analysis and Background Assumptions for the Horndeski Lagrangian

bullet_points: Implement the background field method to expand the Horndeski action to quadratic order in perturbations.
Set up the computation of the one-loop effective action using the heat kernel method, focusing on the relevant Seeley-DeWitt coefficients.
Identify and extract all higher-derivative terms generated at one-loop, especially those that could introduce ghosts.
Ensure the code can handle the constraints $G_{4,X}=0$ and $G_{5,X}=0$ for $c_T^2=1$.
Include explicit validation tests: implement verification routines comparing numerical outputs against known analytical limits or simplified cases.
Document the procedure for isolating higher-derivative terms and outline error estimates for the numerical integration.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Code Implementation and Validation for One-Loop Effective Action Calculation

bullet_points: Analyze the one-loop effective action output from the engineer.
Derive the explicit algebraic/differential relations among the $G_i$ functions that ensure no new ghostly kinetic terms are generated at one-loop.
Formulate the QKSP symmetry conditions and discuss their theoretical implications.
Illustrate the QKSP conditions with concrete examples or special cases of the Horndeski functions to provide clear benchmarks for the ghost-free structure.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Derivation and Illustration of QKSP (Quantum Kinetic Structure Preservation) Symmetry Conditions

bullet_points: For the QKSP-symmetric subclass, numerically solve the background cosmology (Friedmann and scalar field equations).
Compute the evolution of the effective dark energy equation of state, $w_{\text{DE}}$.
Analyze linear scalar perturbations to extract $G_{\text{eff}}$ and the gravitational slip parameter $\eta$.
Clearly specify the numerical methods used for solving the differential equations and discuss convergence criteria.
Include sensitivity analyses to understand how variations in QKSP-symmetric parameters affect the plots of $w_{\text{DE}}(z)$, $G_{\text{eff}}(z)$, and $\eta(z)$.
Provide a summary of the statistical measures used to compare simulation outputs with observational constraints, ensuring the plots are accompanied by error bars or confidence intervals.
Compare these results to current observational constraints (e.g., Planck, LSS, GW170817).
Generate plots of $w_{\text{DE}}(z)$, $G_{\text{eff}}(z)$, and $\eta(z)$ for representative models.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Phenomenological Viability, Numerical Methods, and Observational Constraints

bullet_points: Synthesize the findings from the classical, quantum, and phenomenological analyses.
Carefully cross-reference the results from the classical and one-loop quantum analyses with the numerical outcomes of the phenomenological study.
Discuss the explicit form and implications of the QKSP symmetry.
Interpret the cosmological results and their compatibility with observations.
Reference and interpret the key plots and statistics generated by the engineer.
Discuss any limitations encountered and propose future directions to validate QKSP symmetry under different conditions.
Provide a comprehensive summary of the viability of QKSP-protected Horndeski theories.
